ddls:
CREATE TABLE "particle_systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each particle system */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name given to the particle system */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the particle system's purpose or design */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the system */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the system was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the system was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the particle system (e.g., draft, active, archived) */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the system */,
  "last_modified_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the system was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_particle_systems_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_particle_systems_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., artist, engineer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to (e.g., visual effects, engineering) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged into the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "particle_system_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version of a particle system */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the particle system */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of changes in this version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the version was created */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the version */,
  "approved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the version was approved */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, approved, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_particle_system_versions_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_particle_system_versions_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES particle_systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "simulation_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each set of simulation parameters */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system version these parameters belong to */,
  "density" REAL /* Density of the particles */,
  "triangle_size" INTEGER /* Size of the particles */,
  "transparency" REAL /* Transparency level of the particles */,
  "path_angle" INTEGER /* Angle of the particle paths */,
  "peak_amplitude" REAL /* Peak amplitude of the particle movement */,
  "coherence" REAL /* Coherence level of the particles */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the particles */,
  "lifetime" REAL /* Lifetime of each particle */,
  "emission_rate" REAL /* Emission rate of particles */,
  "gravity" REAL /* Gravitational effect on particles */,
  "wind_force" REAL /* Wind force affecting particles */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_simulation_parameters_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES particle_system_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "simulation_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each simulation result */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system version that was simulated */,
  "run_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the simulation was run */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* JSON object containing performance metrics */,
  "visual_output" TEXT /* File path to the visual output of the simulation */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the simulation was run (e.g., development, production) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any notes or comments on the simulation results */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who ran the simulation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_simulation_results_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES particle_system_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_simulation_results_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaboration" (
  "collab_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration entry */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system being collaborated on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is collaborating */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Level of permission the user has on the particle system */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was assigned to collaborate */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the collaboration (e.g., active, pending, declined) */,
  "expired_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the collaboration expires */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the collaboration request (e.g., review, feedback) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collab_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaboration_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES particle_systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaboration_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system version the comment is on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the comment was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the comment (e.g., active, resolved, deleted) */,
  "parent_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment (for threaded comments) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_parent_comment_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_comment_id") REFERENCES comments ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES particle_system_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system this tag is associated with */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the tag */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tag was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tags_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES particle_systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tags_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who should receive the notification */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system the notification is about */,
  "message" TEXT /* Text of the notification message */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "read_status" TEXT /* Status indicating whether the notification has been read */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., comment, version update, collaboration request) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the notification (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES particle_systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "asset_library" (
  "asset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each asset */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the particle system this asset is used in */,
  "asset_type" TEXT /* Type of the asset (e.g., texture, model) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the asset */,
  "uploaded_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the asset was uploaded */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the asset (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the asset */,
  "file_size" TEXT /* Size of the asset file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("asset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_library_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES particle_systems ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_library_uploaded_by FOREIGN KEY ("uploaded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
